update_every.title: Update every
update_every.description: Data collection interval, measured in seconds.

autodetection_retry.title: Detection retry
autodetection_retry.description: Recheck interval in seconds. Zero means no recheck will be scheduled.

url.title: URL
url.description: The base URL of the VMware vCenter Server.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the HTTP request.

discovery_interval.title: Discovery interval
discovery_interval.description: Hosts, VMs, datastores, clusters, and resource pools discovery interval in seconds.

host_power_states.title: Host power states
host_power_states.description: ESXi host power states to discover. The default preserves existing behavior by discovering only powered-on hosts. Non-powered-on hosts are collected as property/status-only resources; real-time performance counters are skipped.

vm_power_states.title: VM power states
vm_power_states.description: Virtual machine power states to discover. The default preserves existing behavior by discovering only powered-on VMs. Powered-off and suspended VMs are collected as property/status/snapshot-only resources; real-time performance counters are skipped.

collect_inventory_path_label.title: Collect inventory path label
collect_inventory_path_label.description: If enabled, adds an inventory_path chart label to discovered vSphere resources. Disabled by default because inventory paths can expose internal naming schemes.

vm_guest_labels.title: VM guest labels
vm_guest_labels.description: Optional allowlist of VM guest labels to add to VM charts. Disabled by default because guest hostnames, IP addresses, and OS names can be sensitive and high cardinality.

vsphere_tag_categories.title: vSphere tag categories
vsphere_tag_categories.description: Optional glob-pattern allowlist of vSphere tag category names to expose as chart labels. Each list item is one pattern, so names with spaces are supported. Disabled by default because tags are user-defined metadata and can expose internal names. Use "*" only when all tag categories are intentional. Label keys are vsphere_tag_<sanitized_category>; multiple tags in the same category are sorted and joined with "|".

custom_attributes.title: vSphere custom attributes
custom_attributes.description: Optional glob-pattern allowlist of vSphere custom attribute names to expose as chart labels. Each list item is one pattern, so names with spaces are supported. Disabled by default because custom attributes are user-defined metadata and can expose internal names, ownership data, or secrets stored by administrators. Custom attribute values are sent verbatim as labels; never enable patterns that can match secret values. Use "*" only when all custom attributes are intentional. Label keys are vsphere_custom_attribute_<sanitized_name>.

collect_vm_disks.title: Collect VM virtual disks
collect_vm_disks.description: If enabled, collects one capacity series per VM virtual disk. Disabled by default because it can create many time series in large vCenters.

collect_vm_disk_performance.title: Collect VM virtual disk performance
collect_vm_disk_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-virtual-disk I/O, IOPS, latency, and outstanding I/O metrics from vSphere virtualDisk performance instances. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-disk performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

collect_datastore_clusters.title: Collect datastore clusters
collect_datastore_clusters.description: If enabled, collects aggregate capacity and Storage DRS status for datastore clusters (StoragePod objects). Disabled by default because it adds another discovered resource class.

datastore_cluster_include.title: Datastore cluster selector
datastore_cluster_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for datastore clusters when collect_datastore_clusters is enabled. Patterns match /Datacenter/DatastoreCluster, the datastore cluster name, or the vSphere managed object ID.

vm_disk_include.title: VM virtual disk selector
vm_disk_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for VM virtual disks when collect_vm_disks or collect_vm_disk_performance is enabled. Capacity collection patterns match the vSphere disk label, the numeric disk key, or key:<disk_key>. Performance collection patterns match the vSphere performance instance or instance:<disk_instance>.

collect_vm_nic_performance.title: Collect VM network interface performance
collect_vm_nic_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-vNIC traffic, packets, drops, broadcast packets, and multicast packets from vSphere net performance instances. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-interface performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

vm_nic_include.title: VM network interface selector
vm_nic_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for VM network interfaces when collect_vm_nic_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance or interface:<interface_instance>.

collect_host_nic_performance.title: Collect host physical network interface performance
collect_host_nic_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-physical-NIC traffic, packets, drops, errors, broadcast packets, multicast packets, unknown protocol frames, and usage from vSphere net performance instances. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-interface performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

host_nic_include.title: Host physical network interface selector
host_nic_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for host physical network interfaces when collect_host_nic_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance or interface:<interface_instance>.

collect_host_disk_performance.title: Collect host disk device performance
collect_host_disk_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-host-disk/LUN throughput, IOPS, requests, latency, command events, queue depth, and SCSI reservation conflicts from vSphere disk performance instances. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-device performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

host_disk_include.title: Host disk device selector
host_disk_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for host disk/LUN/device instances when collect_host_disk_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance or instance:<disk_instance>.

collect_host_storage_adapter_performance.title: Collect host storage adapter performance
collect_host_storage_adapter_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-host-storage-adapter I/O, command, latency, queue, outstanding I/O percentage, throughput usage, throughput contention, and aggregate maximum latency metrics from vSphere storageAdapter performance counters. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-adapter performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

host_storage_adapter_include.title: Host storage adapter selector
host_storage_adapter_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for host storage adapter instances when collect_host_storage_adapter_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance, adapter:<adapter_instance>, or instance:<adapter_instance>.

collect_host_storage_path_performance.title: Collect host storage path performance
collect_host_storage_path_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-host-storage-path I/O, command, latency, command-event, throughput usage, throughput contention, and aggregate maximum latency metrics from vSphere storagePath performance counters. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-path performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

host_storage_path_include.title: Host storage path selector
host_storage_path_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for host storage path instances when collect_host_storage_path_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance, path:<path_instance>, or instance:<path_instance>.

collect_host_cpu_instance_performance.title: Collect host CPU instance performance
collect_host_cpu_instance_performance.description: If enabled, collects per-host CPU instance usage, utilization, core utilization, used time, and idle time from vSphere cpu performance counters. Disabled by default because it adds high-cardinality per-core/logical-CPU performance series and can increase vCenter query load.

host_cpu_instance_include.title: Host CPU instance selector
host_cpu_instance_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for host CPU instances when collect_host_cpu_instance_performance is enabled. Patterns match the vSphere performance instance, cpu:<cpu_instance>, or instance:<cpu_instance>.

collect_power_metrics.title: Collect power and energy metrics
collect_power_metrics.description: If enabled, collects aggregate host and VM power usage, energy usage, and host power-capacity metrics from vSphere power performance counters. Disabled by default to preserve existing collection scope and avoid extra vCenter performance queries.

collect_vsan.title: Collect vSAN metrics
collect_vsan.description: If enabled, collects vSAN cluster capacity, vSAN cluster health, and vSAN cluster, host, and VM performance metrics through the vSAN Management API. Disabled by default because it requires vSAN APIs and the vSAN Performance Service, and adds additional vCenter queries. Use the vSAN selectors to choose the concrete vSAN performance entity refs queried.

vsan_cluster_include.title: vSAN cluster selector
vsan_cluster_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for vSAN clusters when collect_vsan is enabled. Patterns match /Datacenter/Cluster, the cluster name, the vSphere managed object ID, or vsan_uuid:<uuid>.

vsan_host_include.title: vSAN host selector
vsan_host_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for vSAN host performance entities when collect_vsan is enabled. Patterns match /Datacenter/Cluster/Host, the host name, the vSphere managed object ID, or vsan_node_uuid:<uuid>.

vsan_vm_include.title: vSAN VM selector
vsan_vm_include.description: Simple-pattern allowlist for vSAN VM performance entities when collect_vsan is enabled. Patterns match /Datacenter/Cluster/Host/VM, the VM name, the vSphere managed object ID, or instance_uuid:<uuid>.

collect_network_topology.title: Collect network topology
collect_network_topology.description: If enabled, discovers vSphere Network and Distributed Virtual Port Group objects for the cached vSphere Topology function. Disabled by default to avoid extra vCenter discovery calls for existing users.

not_follow_redirects.title: Not follow redirects
not_follow_redirects.description: If set, the client will not follow HTTP redirects automatically.

vnode.title: Vnode
vnode.description: Associates this data collection job with a [Virtual Node](https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/netdata-agent/configuration/organize-systems-metrics-and-alerts#virtual-nodes).

host_include.title: Host selectors
host_include.description: Configuration for monitoring specific hosts. The selector format follows the pattern `/Datacenter/Cluster/Host`, where each value can be set using [Netdata simple patterns](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/libnetdata/simple_pattern#readme).

vm_include.title: Virtual machine selectors
vm_include.description: Configuration for monitoring specific virtual machines. The selector format follows the pattern `/Datacenter/Cluster/Host/VM`, where each value can be set using [Netdata simple patterns](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/libnetdata/simple_pattern#readme).

datastore_include.title: Datastore selectors
datastore_include.description: Configuration for monitoring specific datastores. The selector format follows the pattern `/Datacenter/Datastore`, where each value can be set using [Netdata simple patterns](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/libnetdata/simple_pattern#readme).

cluster_include.title: Cluster selectors
cluster_include.description: Configuration for monitoring specific clusters and their resource pools. The selector format follows the pattern `/Datacenter/Cluster`, where each value can be set using [Netdata simple patterns](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/libnetdata/simple_pattern#readme). Resource pools follow their owning cluster.

bearer_token_file.title: Bearer Token File
bearer_token_file.description: Path to a file containing a bearer token for HTTP authentication.

force_http2.title: Force HTTP2
force_http2.description: If set, forces the use of HTTP/2 protocol for all requests, even over plain TCP (h2c).

proxy_url.title: Proxy URL
proxy_url.description: The URL of the proxy server.

headers.title: Headers
headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in the request.

tls_skip_verify.title: Skip TLS verification
tls_skip_verify.description: If set, TLS certificate verification will be skipped.

tls_ca.title: TLS CA
tls_ca.description: The path to the CA certificate file for TLS verification.

tls_cert.title: TLS certificate
tls_cert.description: The path to the client certificate file for TLS authentication.

tls_key.title: TLS key
tls_key.description: The path to the client key file for TLS authentication.

bearer_token_file.ui:help: The token is sent in the Authorization header as `Bearer <token>`. **Takes priority over basic authentication**.

autodetection_retry.ui:help: This option determines how frequently (in seconds) Netdata will retry data collection jobs that failed initially, with the value of 60 meaning it retries to start data collection jobs every 60 seconds, while setting it to 0 disables this retry mechanism entirely.

vnode.ui:placeholder: To use this option, first create a Virtual Node and then reference its name here.

update_every.ui:help: **Important**: vSphere generates real-time statistics every 20 seconds. Setting this value lower won't improve data accuracy. For larger vSphere deployments, consider increasing this value to ensure complete data collection during each cycle. To find the optimal value, run the collector in debug mode and see how long it takes to collect metrics.

timeout.ui:help: Accepts decimals for precise control (e.g., type 1.5 for 1.5 seconds).
